message:
fix(pages): switch recovery secret encoding from base64 to base64url and add normalizeBase64 to crypto worker

Recovery secrets were encoded with standard base64 (using + and /) but QR code
scanning and URL contexts can corrupt these characters. This switches to base64url
encoding via RecoverySecret::encoded() which uses URL-safe characters (- and _).

bundle.rs:
- write_private_artifacts_encrypted() now uses RecoverySecret::from_bytes()
  followed by .encoded() instead of raw BASE64_STANDARD.encode()
- Added entropy validation context for clearer error messages
- Updated recovery-secret.txt label from "Secret (base64)" to "Secret (base64url)"
- Removed unused base64::prelude import

crypto_worker.js:
- New normalizeBase64() function converts base64url to standard base64 by
  replacing - with + and _ with /, then adding padding as needed
- base64ToArray() now runs input through normalizeBase64() before atob(),
  ensuring the web viewer can decode both old (standard) and new (base64url)
  encoded recovery secrets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
